sections.name: Basin Touch Ritornello
journey: handpan touch teaches the pulse-body before the voice world exists
destination: a basin of attack, field, resonance, and withheld cadence

harmony: D Kurd basin: Ding, A/Bb/C/D fields, harp fifths, viola/bass-clarinet afterimage
process: the handpan starts as physical touch, harp widens the decay, and the low instruments arrive only after the first negative space

checks: handpan identity is Ding plus field answer, not tune
opening negative space
ritornello closes as residue, not cadence

gestures.idea: Ding, tonefield, touch, and decay are the material
mechanism: handpan bars alternate attack and negative space while harp answers after the attack

line_relations.text: pan supplies touch; harp supplies widened resonance

sections.name: A: Soprano Paragraph
journey: the upper voice discovers a singable paragraph inside the basin
destination: the soprano leaves a tail that the ritornello can inherit

harmony: D minor basin with Bb color; low support enters only as distant breath
process: soprano states the A behavior plainly while handpan sustains a soft two-hand shimmer underneath it

checks: A subject starts as vocal syntax over cleared accompaniment
first clause breath has support but not cadence

harmony: answer turns through D, G, C, A, and back toward D without a boxed cadence
process: the soprano delays the downbeat while handpan shifts its two-hand shimmer upward and viola becomes the short inner respondent

checks: second clause changes phrase grammar with held G5 and delayed answer

harmony: D and Bb colors loosen into an A/E/D handoff
process: the soprano phrase does not cadence; its tail is made available to viola and harp before S3

checks: soprano tail is handed off instead of cadencing

gestures.idea: A is a vocal paragraph, not repeated four-bar boxes
mechanism: clause 1 states, clause 2 delays the downbeat, clause 3 leaves a transferable tail

line_relations.text: the S2 ending gives the tail to viola before handpan returns

sections.name: Vocalized Ritornello
journey: the basin returns after being changed by the soprano tail
destination: a compressed ritornello leaves space for basso to become foreground

harmony: D field with Bb residue and C#/B pressure beginning to appear
process: viola and harp begin from the soprano residue; handpan enters late and dry

checks: ritornello is dovetailed, not reset
outside pressure opens toward basso paragraph

sections.name: B: Basso Paragraph
journey: the lower voice becomes foreground and moves outside the basin
destination: dynamic pressure peaks in the basso line and then opens a hole into the exposed hinge

harmony: D/A frame with darker E and Bb supports
process: basso establishes B in front while handpan becomes a short, quiet low-pulse instead of decoration

checks: basso is melody, not bass function

harmony: B begins to move outside handpan inventory through C# and B natural
process: basso rises and compresses while soprano memories are isolated, not a duet yet

checks: outside color is assigned away from handpan

harmony: C, B, C# pressure peaks, then sound falls away before the hinge
process: the dynamic maximum is in the basso's compressed line; the end opens a hole instead of cadencing

checks: dynamic maximum is in the basso line, not added percussion
end of B opens a hole into S5

sections.name: Two-Voice Cadenza Hinge
journey: the vessel nearly disappears so the two vocal materials can survive exposed
destination: an incomplete inhalation into the duet return

harmony: A/B fragments expose C# and B natural without handpan cover
process: soprano and basso answer each other almost bare; handpan appears only as one anchor and one withheld touch

checks: hinge begins as exposed antiphony
outside color remains vocal and naked
return is inhaled, not confirmed

sections.name: A': Shared-Breath Duet
journey: A returns as interdependent duet grammar rather than enlarged reprise
destination: the shared-breath destination arrives in bars 67-72, before the saved soprano high point

harmony: D basin returns cautiously after the incomplete hinge
process: voices resume first, then handpan returns as two-hand shimmer; neither voice accompanies the other

checks: A' begins with voices, not full ensemble reset

harmony: A, G, Bb, E colors thin before the shared destination
process: the duet approaches by suspension and clearing, not continuous buildup

checks: bar before destination clears rather than builds

harmony: D, F, G, A, and C colors hold the voices in shared syntax
process: the destination is two vocal lines completing one another while handpan supplies selective floor

checks: formal destination is shared breath, not loud climax
destination resolves as mutual syntax

gestures.idea: A' is two foreground lines discovering one breath
mechanism: soprano and basso alternate holds, answers, contrary motion, and offset rests

line_relations.text: neither voice doubles or merely supports the other in bars 67-72

harmony: D basin opens wide after the destination
process: the saved soprano A5 is release over low space, then the ensemble reduces toward afterimage

checks: saved high point is release after destination
release reduces toward afterimage

sections.name: After-Ritornello
journey: the singing leaves the vessel behind
destination: one final Ding and suspended harp color remain after the aria

harmony: D/A/E suspension, without a full cadential roll
process: only one soprano residue appears; handpan and harp become a composite decay

checks: only one vocal residue remains
final state is composite decay, not recap

gestures.idea: the aria leaves touch and resonance behind
mechanism: only one vocal hum remains; the final handpan Ding enters after harp has already suspended D/A/E

line_relations.text: last attack and long decay form the final sonority
